title: Open Scout API — shared definitions
description: Shared $defs for all canonical dataset schemas: identifiers, historical dates, provenance, the temporal version envelope, and lifecycle events. See PLAN.md §3 for the model narrative. Canonical-layer schemas are strict (unevaluatedProperties/additionalProperties: false); published projections get their own, looser schemas later.

Slug.title: Slug
Slug.description: Permanent, dataset-scoped entity identifier. Never reused, never deleted, never encodes mutable facts (e.g. council number). Disambiguate historical collisions with an era suffix (e.g. 'black-hawk-1935').

EntityRef.title: EntityRef
EntityRef.description: Cross-dataset reference: '{kind}:{slug}'. Known kinds: council, territory, camp, merit-badge, requirement-set; future kinds (rank, award, oa-lodge) use the same shape. Referential integrity is a pipeline rule, not a schema rule.

HistoricalDate.title: HistoricalDate
HistoricalDate.description: ISO 8601 date at reduced precision: 'YYYY', 'YYYY-MM', or 'YYYY-MM-DD'. Real history is often only known to the year.

Source.title: Source
Source.description: One checkable source for a fact. At least one of url/citation is required; prefer url. 'citation' covers offline sources (books, announcements, requirement pamphlet editions).

citation.description: Free-text citation for offline or ephemeral sources.

accessed.description: Full ISO date the source was last consulted.

Method.title: Method
Method.description: How the fact entered the dataset. Merge precedence (pipeline rule): curated/official_publication > community > scraped > llm_extraction. 'imported' = bulk import from a sibling project (e.g. camp-finder) carrying that project's provenance.

Provenance.title: Provenance
Provenance.description: Required on every version record, event, and requirement set. LLM-extracted facts MUST declare confidence < 1.0.

verified_at.description: Full ISO date a human/pipeline last confirmed this record against its sources.

imported_at.description: Date this record was ingested into open-scout-api, distinct from verified_at (the source's own confirmation date). Present on imported records.

VersionBase.title: VersionBase
VersionBase.description: Envelope for one effective-dated snapshot of an entity's attributes (record-level SCD-2). valid_from null = unknown start; valid_to null = current. Pipeline rules (not schema-expressible): versions within an entity are chronologically ordered and non-overlapping; retired entities have no null-valid_to version.

valid_from.description: When this snapshot became true in the world; null = unknown.

valid_to.description: When this snapshot stopped being true; null = still current.

EventType.title: EventType
EventType.description: Lifecycle event vocabulary, shared across datasets. Domain mapping: council founded / camp opened / badge introduced -> 'established'; camp closed / badge retired / council dissolved -> 'discontinued'; badge replaced by a new badge -> 'superseded'; camp sold or moved between councils -> 'transferred'; 2021 regions->territories -> 'reorganized'; one entity absorbing another (absorber keeps identity) -> 'absorbed'.

ParticipantRole.title: ParticipantRole
ParticipantRole.description: 'subject' for single-entity events (renamed, established, discontinued, reinstated); 'predecessor'/'successor' for identity-changing events (merged, split, superseded, reorganized); 'continuing' for the surviving side of 'absorbed' and the receiving side of 'transferred'.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: One lifecycle event, stored exactly once in the dataset's _events.json (never duplicated into entity files; the build step projects events into published entity JSON). Pipeline rules: event ids unique per dataset; every participant ref resolves; date consistent with participant version boundaries.

date.description: When the event took effect; null = unknown.

VocabCode.title: VocabCode
VocabCode.description: Open-vocabulary code (snake_case). New codes are data changes, recorded in a registry file (future: data/vocab/), not schema changes.
